strands.title: Working Directory State
strands.content: User has established /home/tony/projects as a working directory in cycle 1. This binding remains active unless explicitly modified.

strands.title: Model Configuration
strands.content: User issued /model command in cycle 2 (turn 5), selecting claude-opus-4-6 (Opus). Cycle 3 (turn 8-9): User reissued /model command with identical argument (claude-opus-4-6), confirming or reaffirming Opus as the active model. This repeated selection reinforces Opus as the stable, intentional model choice.
key_claims: claude-opus-4-6 is the confirmed active model across cycles 2 and 3
User preference for Opus appears deliberate (not accidental, given repetition)

declared_losses.why: Original context from cycle 1 did not contain explicit intent articulation; remains unresolved

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specifics of prior model configuration before cycle 2
declared_losses.why: No baseline model state recorded in cycle 1 tensor

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Context or motivation for cycle 3 /model reissue
declared_losses.why: Local command caveat excludes turn 7 from consideration; turn 8-9 show command + confirmation but no accompanying user explanation of why the command was repeated

open_questions: What is the user's intent for adding ~/projects as a working directory? (carries forward from cycle 1)
Does the user expect this directory to persist across sessions or is it temporary? (carries forward from cycle 1)
What role should permissions management play in subsequent operations? (carries forward from cycle 1)
Why did the user switch to Opus model in cycle 2 — is this for a specific task requirement or exploration?
Why did the user reissue the /model command with identical arguments in cycle 3 — was this a confirmation, a test, or accidental repetition?
Will the reaffirmed Opus model be used for a specific reasoning task in the next cycle?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "Maintain binding of ~/projects to /home/tony/projects — active working directory context across all cycles",
  "Opus (claude-opus-4-6) is confirmed as the stable active model; treat this as a firm preference unless contradicted by future /model commands",
  "If user references file operations, use /home/tony/projects as the context root unless explicitly overridden",
  "Monitor for /add-dir, /remove-dir, /set-working-dir, or /model commands that would update state",
  "If the next user turn contains substantive content (not local commands), integrate it into relevant strands and update epistemic values accordingly",
  "The repeated /model command in cycle 3 suggests stability in configuration — expect this context to remain unless user signals change",
  "If user provides intent clarification for ~/projects, task context, or explains the /model reissue, integrate into respective strands and elevate open question priorities"
]
